findings.claim: The SST-IRES-Cre mouse line labels 6-10% fast-spiking/parvalbumin-expressing interneurons as off-target recombination, reaching up to 14% in layer 4 of S1
findings.claim_source_sentence: Of these, on average 6% were immunopositive for PV. percentage of RFP+/PV+ double-labeled cells was highest in layer 4 of S1, reaching 14% in one animal and averaging 11.5 and 10% in the younger and older animals, respectively.
findings.effect_size: 6-14% off-target PV labeling in SST-Cre line
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Of these, on average 6% were immunopositive for PV. percentage of RFP+/PV+ double-labeled cells was highest in layer 4 of S1, reaching 14% in one animal
findings.study_system: Mouse somatosensory cortex, SST-IRES-Cre x Ai14

findings.claim: A toolbox of 4 Cre-dependent optogenetic transgenic mouse lines (ChR2-tdTomato, ChR2-EYFP, eNpHR3.0, Arch-ER2) enabling robust cell-type-specific activation or silencing when combined with Cre driver lines
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we present a toolbox of 4 knock-in mouse lines engineered for strong, Cre-dependent expression of channelrhodopsins ChR2-tdTomato and ChR2-EYFP, halorhodopsin eNpHR3.0, and archaerhodopsin Arch-ER2.
findings.effect_size: ChR2-EYFP and Arch-ER2 approaching sensitivity of in utero or virally transduced neurons
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: with ChR2-EYFP and Arch-ER2 demonstrating light sensitivity approaching that of in utero or virally transduced neurons
findings.study_system: Mouse cortex, Cre-dependent transgenic lines

findings.claim: Germline recombination occurs in over half of 64 commonly used Cre driver lines, with parental sex bias; reporters are not reliable proxies for other loci of interest
findings.claim_source_sentence: Collated data reveal germline recombination in over half of 64 commonly used Cre driver lines, in most cases with a parental sex bias related to Cre expression in sperm or oocytes. Specific target loci demonstrated differential recombination; thus, reporters are not reliable proxies for another locus of interest.
findings.effect_size: Over half (>50%) of 64 Cre lines show germline recombination
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Collated data reveal germline recombination in over half of 64 commonly used Cre driver lines
findings.study_system: Mouse, 64 commonly used Cre driver lines

findings.claim: Intersectional Cre/Flp and Cre/Dre double recombinase strategies with the TIGRE locus enable substantially higher expression levels of genetic tools with increased specificity in neuronal subtypes
findings.claim_source_sentence: We developed driver and double reporter mouse lines and viral vectors using the Cre/Flp and Cre/Dre double recombinase systems, and established a new, retargetable genomic locus, TIGRE, which allowed the generation of a large set of Cre/tTA dependent reporter lines expressing fluorescent proteins, genetically encoded calcium, voltage, or glutamate indicators, and optogenetic effectors, all at substantially higher levels than before.
findings.study_system: Mouse, TIGRE locus transgenic lines

findings.claim: TIGRE2.0 reporter platform broke the barrier in transgene expression level of single-copy targeted-insertion transgenesis across a wide range of neuronal types, with 23 new driver lines and 26 new reporter lines
findings.claim_source_sentence: TIGRE2.0 reporters broke the barrier in transgene expression level of single-copy targeted-insertion transgenesis in a wide range of neuronal types, along with additional advantage of a simplified breeding strategy compared to our first-generation TIGRE lines.
findings.effect_size: 23 new driver lines, 26 new reporter lines
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Here we report a large set of driver and reporter transgenic mouse lines, including 23 new driver lines targeting a variety of cortical and subcortical cell populations and 26 new reporter lines expressing an array of molecular tools.
findings.study_system: Mouse, TIGRE2.0 transgenic platform

findings.claim: A substantial proportion of layer 5 PV neurons in the PV-Cre/tdTomato mouse line are excitatory (not inhibitory); the intersectional Vgat-Cre/PV-Flp/tdTomato line shows no colocalization with Vglut1, providing more reliable targeting of GABAergic PV interneurons
findings.claim_source_sentence: a substantial proportion of layer 5 PV neurons was found to be excitatory and not inhibitory in the PV-Cre/tdTomato mouse. The intersectional transgenic mouse line Vgat-Cre/PV-Flp/tdTomato provided a solution, since no colocalization of tdTomato with the Vglut1 probe was found there.
findings.study_system: Mouse cortex, PV-Cre/tdTomato and Vgat-Cre/PV-Flp/tdTomato lines

findings.claim: Channelrhodopsin-2 enables millisecond-timescale, genetically targeted optical control of neural activity, establishing the foundation for optogenetic circuit interrogation
findings.claim_source_sentence: Millisecond-timescale, genetically targeted optical control of neural activity
findings.study_system: Rat hippocampal neurons in culture

findings.claim: Systematic comparison of microbial opsins under matched conditions revealed that performance depends critically on vector, promoter, expression time, illumination, and cell type, making direct comparisons across studies problematic
findings.claim_source_sentence: This challenge has been compounded by the dependence of performance on parameters such as vector, promoter, expression time, illumination, cell type and many other variables. Here we systematically compared microbial opsins under matched experimental conditions to extract essential principles and identify key parameters.
findings.study_system: Mouse cortical neurons, multiple opsin variants

findings.claim: Chronos has faster kinetics than any previous channelrhodopsin yet is more light-sensitive; Chrimson is 45 nm red-shifted relative to all prior channelrhodopsins, enabling crosstalk-free two-color activation of independent neural populations
findings.claim_source_sentence: Chrimson is 45 nm red-shifted relative to any previous channelrhodopsin. Chronos has faster kinetics than any previous channelrhodopsin, yet is effectively more light-sensitive. Together, these two reagents enable crosstalk-free two-color activation of neural spiking in independent neural populations in mouse brain slice.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Chrimson is 45 nm red-shifted relative to any previous channelrhodopsin
findings.study_system: Mouse brain slice, multiple algal opsins from >100 species

findings.claim: The red-shifted cruxhalorhodopsin Jaws produces photocurrents three times those of earlier silencers and enables noninvasive transcranial optical inhibition deep in the brains of awake mice
findings.claim_source_sentence: We present a red-shifted cruxhalorhodopsin, Jaws, derived from Haloarcula salinarum and engineered to result in red light-induced photocurrents three times those of earlier silencers. We also demonstrate that Jaws can noninvasively mediate transcranial optical inhibition of neurons deep in the brains of awake mice.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: engineered to result in red light-induced photocurrents three times those of earlier silencers
findings.study_system: Mouse cortex in vivo, retinitis pigmentosa model mice

findings.claim: Over the first decade of optogenetics (2005-2015), the convergence of microbial opsin engineering, genetic targeting methods, and optical strategies enabled spatiotemporally precise causal control over cellular signaling, with thousands of publications using these tools
findings.claim_source_sentence: Over the past 10 years, the development and convergence of microbial opsin engineering, modular genetic methods for cell-type targeting and optical strategies for guiding light through tissue have enabled versatile optical control of defined cells in living systems.
findings.study_system: Review of optogenetics field 2005-2015

findings.claim: Silencing strategies vary widely in mechanism of action, spatial and temporal scales; design and interpretation of neuronal silencing experiments present unique challenges both technically and conceptually
findings.claim_source_sentence: Silencing strategies vary widely in their mechanism of action and in their spatial and temporal scales. Although such manipulations are commonly applied, the design and interpretation of neuronal silencing experiments present unique challenges, both technically and conceptually.
findings.study_system: Review of silencing tools and approaches

findings.claim: eOPN3 (mosquito rhodopsin homolog) effectively suppresses synaptic transmission through the Gi/o pathway; brief illumination triggers lasting suppression that recovers spontaneously within minutes, overcoming the low efficacy and off-target effects of existing inhibitory opsins at presynaptic terminals
findings.claim_source_sentence: existing inhibitory optogenetic tools have low efficacy and off-target effects when applied to presynaptic terminals. eOPN3 can effectively suppress synaptic transmission through the Gi/o signaling pathway. Brief illumination of presynaptic terminals expressing eOPN3 triggers a lasting suppression of synaptic output that recovers spontaneously within minutes in vitro and in vivo.
findings.study_system: Mouse nigrostriatal dopamine axons, in vivo and in vitro

findings.claim: Two-photon holographic amplified laser illumination of Chronos evokes spikes with submillisecond precision and repeated firing up to 100 Hz in brain slices, achieving the temporal sophistication needed to mimic natural microcircuit activity patterns
findings.claim_source_sentence: 2P holographic amplified laser illumination of Chronos can evoke spikes with submillisecond precision and repeated firing up to 100 Hz in brain slices. These results pave the way for optogenetic manipulation with the spatial and temporal sophistication necessary to mimic natural microcircuit activity.
findings.effect_size: Submillisecond precision, up to 100 Hz firing
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: can evoke spikes with submillisecond precision and repeated firing up to 100 Hz in brain slices
findings.study_system: Mouse brain slices, Swiss male mice, Chronos opsin

findings.claim: Optogenetics enables cell-type-specific interrogation of neural systems with high temporal precision, but interpretive challenges remain regarding the relationship between artificial stimulation and natural circuit dynamics
findings.claim_source_sentence: Optogenetics in neural systems
findings.study_system: Review of optogenetics applications

findings.claim: DREADDs (Designer Receptors Exclusively Activated by Designer Drugs) were created by evolving muscarinic receptors to be activated solely by the pharmacologically inert compound CNO; the Gi-coupled hM4D silences hippocampal neurons through membrane hyperpolarization
findings.claim_source_sentence: We evolved muscarinic receptors in yeast to generate a family of G protein-coupled receptors that are activated solely by a pharmacologically inert drug-like and bioavailable compound (clozapine-N-oxide). We also expressed a Gi-coupled designer receptor in hippocampal neurons (hM4D) and demonstrated its ability to induce membrane hyperpolarization and neuronal silencing.
findings.study_system: Yeast, human cell lines, hippocampal neurons

findings.claim: CNO (clozapine N-oxide) is reverse-metabolized to clozapine in both rats and mice, and produces clozapine-like interoceptive stimulus effects; at DREADD-activating doses (10 mg/kg), full substitution for clozapine was detected in some animals
findings.claim_source_sentence: Following administration of 10.0 mg/kg CNO, pharmacokinetic analyses replicated recent reports of back-conversion to clozapine in rats and revealed that this phenomenon also occurs in mice. CNO produced partial substitution for the clozapine stimulus on average, with full substitution being detected in some individual animals of both species at doses frequently used to activate DREADDs.
findings.effect_size: 10 mg/kg CNO produces partial to full clozapine substitution
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Following administration of 10.0 mg/kg CNO, pharmacokinetic analyses replicated recent reports of back-conversion to clozapine in rats and revealed that this phenomenon also occurs in mice.
findings.study_system: Rats and mice, drug discrimination paradigm

findings.claim: Deschloroclozapine (DCZ) is a potent, selective, and metabolically stable DREADD agonist that activates DREADDs within minutes at low doses (1-3 μg/kg) in both mice and monkeys, overcoming the off-target and metabolic limitations of CNO
findings.claim_source_sentence: Systemic delivery of low doses of DCZ (1 or 3 μg per kg) enhanced neuronal activity via hM3Dq within minutes in mice and monkeys. Intramuscular injections of DCZ (100 μg per kg) reversibly induced spatial working memory deficits in monkeys expressing hM4Di in the prefrontal cortex.
findings.effect_size: 1-3 μg/kg DCZ activates DREADDs within minutes; 100 μg/kg reversibly impairs working memory
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Systemic delivery of low doses of DCZ (1 or 3 μg per kg) enhanced neuronal activity via hM3Dq within minutes in mice and monkeys.
findings.study_system: Mouse and monkey, hM3Dq and hM4Di DREADDs

findings.claim: DREADDs have become ubiquitously used to modulate GPCR activity noninvasively in vivo, directly implicating GPCR signaling in numerous therapeutically relevant contexts, but the temporal resolution remains limited to minutes-to-hours timescales
findings.claim_source_sentence: DREADDs are now used ubiquitously to modulate GPCR activity noninvasively in vivo. Results from these studies have directly implicated GPCR signaling in a large number of therapeutically relevant contexts.
findings.study_system: Review of DREADD technology applications

findings.claim: DREADD-mediated activation of PV+ interneurons in somatosensory cortex or reticular thalamic nucleus provided anti-epileptic effects against PTZ-induced absence seizures; CNO activation of PV FFI either prevented or suppressed seizure severity
findings.claim_source_sentence: DREADD-mediated activation of PV+ interneurons provided anti-epileptic effects against PTZ-induced seizures. CNO activation of FFI either prevented PTZ-induced absence seizures or suppressed their severity.
findings.study_system: Mouse, PV-Cre/Gq-DREADD, PTZ-induced seizures

findings.claim: Elevating the cellular excitation/inhibition balance in medial prefrontal cortex by expressing a step-function opsin in pyramidal neurons impaired information processing and was associated with behavioral phenotypes relevant to psychiatric disease
findings.claim_source_sentence: Neocortical excitation/inhibition balance in information processing and social dysfunction
findings.study_system: Mouse medial prefrontal cortex

findings.claim: The 'optoclamp' provides continuous, real-time bidirectional optical stimulation to lock spiking activity at specified targets over timescales from seconds to days, decoupling neuronal firing from ongoing changes in network excitability
findings.claim_source_sentence: we present the 'optoclamp', a feedback control technology that provides continuous, real-time adjustments of bidirectional optical stimulation in order to lock spiking activity at specified targets over timescales ranging from seconds to days. We demonstrate how this system can be used to decouple neuronal firing levels from ongoing changes in network excitability.
findings.effect_size: Control over timescales from seconds to days
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: lock spiking activity at specified targets over timescales ranging from seconds to days
findings.study_system: Mouse barrel cortex in vivo and cortical cultures in vitro

findings.claim: Closing the loop optogenetically—basing stimulation on simultaneously observed dynamics—is a powerful strategy for causal investigation, particularly for testing whether inferred models of dynamics, connectivity, and causation are accurate in vivo
findings.claim_source_sentence: Closing the loop optogenetically (i.e., basing optogenetic stimulation on simultaneously observed dynamics in a principled way) is a powerful strategy for causal investigation of neural circuitry. observing and feeding back the effects of circuit interventions on physiologically relevant timescales is valuable for directly testing whether inferred models of dynamics, connectivity, and causation are accurate in vivo.
findings.study_system: Review of closed-loop optogenetics approaches

findings.claim: Simultaneous two-photon optogenetic activation and calcium imaging with cellular resolution in vivo, enabled by coexpression of red-shifted opsin and GCaMP, permits targeting of tens of user-selected neurons with negligible optical crosstalk
findings.claim_source_sentence: Concurrent two-photon optogenetic activation and calcium imaging is enabled by coexpression of a red-shifted opsin and a genetically encoded calcium indicator. A spatial light modulator allows tens of user-selected neurons to be targeted for spatiotemporally precise optogenetic activation, while simultaneous fast calcium imaging provides high-resolution network-wide readout of the manipulation with negligible optical crosstalk.
findings.effect_size: Tens of neurons targeted with single-cell and single-spike resolution
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: A spatial light modulator allows tens of user-selected neurons to be targeted for spatiotemporally precise optogenetic activation
findings.study_system: Mouse barrel cortex in vivo

findings.claim: High-efficiency all-optical interrogation using two-photon holographic methods enables probing of neural circuitry with single-cell resolution in behaving animals
findings.claim_source_sentence: Optogenetic strategies for high-efficiency all-optical interrogation using two-photon holographic methods
findings.study_system: Mouse cortex, two-photon holographic stimulation

findings.claim: Intersectional monosynaptic tracing dissects subtype-specific organization of inputs to defined neuronal populations, providing circuit-level specificity beyond what single-recombinase approaches can achieve
findings.claim_source_sentence: Intersectional monosynaptic tracing for dissecting subtype-specific organization of inputs
findings.study_system: Mouse, intersectional viral tracing

findings.claim: Highly specific targeting can be achieved through recombinase-dependent AAVs in combination with transgenic rodent lines that express Cre-recombinase in specific cell types. Visualization of viral expression is typically achieved through fluorescent reporter proteins (e.g., GFP or mCherry) packaged
findings.claim_source_sentence: Highly specific targeting can be achieved through recombinase-dependent AAVs in combination with transgenic rodent lines that express Cre-recombinase in specific cell types. Visualization of viral expression is typically achieved through fluorescent reporter proteins (e.g., GFP or mCherry) packaged
findings.study_system: Off-target expression of Cre-dependent adeno-associated viruses in wild type C57BL/6J mice

findings.claim: In single-transfected animals, immunohistochemistry demonstrated strong expression of either the α6 or δ subunit; in cotransfected animals, both subunits were consistently expressed in the same neurons. Electrophysiology revealed a robust increase of tonic current, with progressively larger increase
findings.claim_source_sentence: In single-transfected animals, immunohistochemistry demonstrated strong expression of either the α6 or δ subunit; in cotransfected animals, both subunits were consistently expressed in the same neurons. Electrophysiology revealed a robust increase of tonic current, with progressively larger increase
findings.study_system: Ectopic Expression of α6 and δ GABAA Receptor Subunits in Hilar Somatostatin Neurons Increases Tonic

The methodology described here should provide a framework for future
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that ChR2 expression was insufficient in PV-Cre mouse at PN day 10 (PN10) and that this channel needs to be expressed from embryonic stages (as in the Nkx2.1-Cre line) to allow for a reliable photoactivation in mouse pups. The methodology described here should provide a framework for future

findings.claim: Previously, we demonstrated that loss of excitatory AMPA-receptors, specifically at synapses on PV+ interneurons in CTC feedforward microcircuits, occurs in the stargazer mouse model of absence epilepsy. No significant changes were observed in vehicle-treated or non-DREADD wild-type control animals.

findings.claim: In each case, imaging in brain slices from male or female animals revealed electrically evoked optical signals from multiple individual neurons in single trials. These imaging experiments revealed action potentials, dynamic aspects of dendritic integration, and trial-to-trial fluctuations in respons
findings.claim_source_sentence: In each case, imaging in brain slices from male or female animals revealed electrically evoked optical signals from multiple individual neurons in single trials. findings.claim: An established paradigm to study spike encoding in controlled conditions in vitro uses intracellular injection of a mixture of signals with fluctuating currents that mimic in vivo-like background activity. Recent progress in optogenetics provides an opportunity to circumvent these limitations.

findings.claim: Using electroencephalography (EEG) and electromyography (EMG) to analyse sleep, we found a dose-dependent suppression of rapid eye movement (REM) sleep, changes in EEG spectral power during non-REM (NREM) sleep, and altered sleep architecture in a pattern previously reported for clozapine. findings.claim_source_sentence: Using electroencephalography (EEG) and electromyography (EMG) to analyse sleep, we found a dose-dependent suppression of rapid eye movement (REM) sleep, changes in EEG spectral power during non-REM (NREM) sleep, and altered sleep architecture in a pattern previously reported for clozapine. Interesti
findings.study_system: Effects of clozapine-N-oxide and compound 21 on sleep in laboratory mice.

findings.claim: However, several DREADD ligands, e.g., clozapine-N-oxide (CNO), have metabolic and off-target effects that may confound experimental findings. Together, our findings support the use of systemic DCZ for DREADD-based manipulaations in rats, and provide evidence that DCZ is a superior alternative to CN
findings.claim_source_sentence: However, several DREADD ligands, e.g., clozapine-N-oxide (CNO), have metabolic and off-target effects that may confound experimental findings. Together, our findings support the use of systemic DCZ for DREADD-based manipulaations in rats, and provide evidence that DCZ is a superior alternative to CN

findings.claim: CLZ at 1 mg/kg evoked a stronger and longer-lasting neural response but produced off-target effects, observed as changes in locomotor behavior and FDG-microPET imaging.
findings.claim_source_sentence: CLZ at 1 mg/kg evoked a stronger and longer-lasting neural response but produced off-target effects, observed as changes in locomotor behavior and FDG-microPET imaging.

conflicts.description: CNO pharmacological inertness: The original DREADD paper claimed CNO is pharmacologically inert, but multiple subsequent studies demonstrated CNO is reverse-metabolized to clozapine in vivo and produces off-target behavioral and neural effects at commonly used doses
conflicts.paper_a_claim: CNO is a pharmacologically inert drug-like and bioavailable compound suitable for selective GPCR activation
conflicts.paper_b_claim: CNO is reverse-metabolized to clozapine and produces clozapine-like interoceptive stimulus effects in both rats and mice at doses frequently used to activate DREADDs
conflicts.resolution_notes: Multiple independent studies confirmed CNO back-conversion. Field has moved to alternative agonists (DCZ, compound 21). CNO can still be used with appropriate controls.

conflicts.description: SST-Cre line specificity: Whether the SST-IRES-Cre line provides sufficiently specific targeting of SST interneurons for optogenetic and functional studies
conflicts.paper_a_claim: 6-10% of neurons expressing Cre-dependent reporter in SST-Cre mice are fast-spiking/PV+ interneurons, potentially affecting results of studies using this line
conflicts.paper_b_claim: 5% off-target recombination in visual cortex SST-Cre mice was found but considered tolerable for most circuit-level studies
conflicts.resolution_notes: Off-target rate confirmed by multiple groups. Whether this rate meaningfully affects conclusions depends on the specific experiment. Intersectional strategies provide higher specificity when needed.

conflicts.description: Gain-of-function vs loss-of-function interpretive framework: Whether optogenetic activation experiments can establish normal function of a cell type
conflicts.paper_a_claim: Optogenetic activation establishes causal sufficiency of defined cell types for specific circuit functions
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Silencing and activation experiments probe different questions; activation demonstrates sufficiency but not necessity, and non-physiological activation patterns can produce artifacts not reflecting normal function
conflicts.resolution_notes: The field increasingly recognizes that both approaches have interpretive limitations. Multi-method convergence is the emerging standard.

conflicts.description: Germline recombination in Cre lines: Whether germline recombination is a minor or major concern for Cre-dependent experiments
conflicts.paper_a_claim: Germline recombination occurs in over half of 64 commonly used Cre driver lines, with reporters not being reliable proxies for other loci
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Cre-dependent optogenetic transgenic mice provide robust, consistent, and inducible expression with unprecedented reliability and accuracy when combined with Cre driver lines
conflicts.resolution_notes: Both claims are correct but address different aspects. The toolbox works well when properly validated, but germline recombination means breeding protocols must be carefully designed with appropriate genotyping controls.

conflicts.description: PV-Cre line labeling specificity in deep layers: Whether PV-Cre labels only GABAergic interneurons or also excitatory neurons in cortical layer 5
conflicts.paper_a_claim: A substantial proportion of layer 5 PV neurons in PV-Cre/tdTomato mice are excitatory (Vglut1+), not inhibitory
conflicts.paper_b_claim: PV-Cre mice show specific photoactivation of parvalbumin-positive interneurons in behaving animals
conflicts.resolution_notes: The intersectional Vgat-Cre/PV-Flp/tdTomato line is proposed as a solution. This finding needs independent replication and quantification across cortical areas.

figure_data.comparison_description: Comparison of off-target recombination percentages reported for commonly used interneuron Cre driver lines across studies

papers.value_source_sentence: Of these, on average 6% were immunopositive for PV

papers.condition: Germline recombination rate across 64 Cre lines
papers.n_definition: Number of Cre driver lines tested

figure_data.homogeneity_check: CAUTION: These measurements are not directly comparable. The first two measure ectopic somatic recombination (off-target cell type labeling) while the third measures germline recombination (unintended global deletion). Different metrics, different biological mechanisms.
figure_data.n_definition: Varies: neurons examined vs Cre lines tested

figure_data.comparison_title: Opsin Kinetics and Performance: Evolution of Channelrhodopsin Tools
figure_data.comparison_description: Comparison of temporal precision and spectral shift across major opsin variants used in interneuron studies
figure_data.metric: Maximum reliable firing frequency (Hz) or spectral shift (nm)

papers.value_source_sentence: Millisecond-timescale, genetically targeted optical control of neural activity
Chrimson is 45 nm red-shifted relative to any previous channelrhodopsin
can evoke spikes with submillisecond precision and repeated firing up to 100 Hz in brain slices
engineered to result in red light-induced photocurrents three times those of earlier silencers
papers.condition: Jaws photocurrent fold-increase over prior silencers

figure_data.homogeneity_check: CAUTION: Entries mix different metrics (firing frequency in Hz, spectral shift in nm, fold-change in photocurrent). Not a single comparable axis. Useful as a timeline of tool development capabilities but not for direct numerical comparison.

figure_data.comparison_title: DREADD Actuator Selectivity: CNO vs DCZ
figure_data.comparison_description: Comparison of DREADD agonist doses and off-target profiles: traditional CNO vs next-generation DCZ

papers.value_source_sentence: activated solely by a pharmacologically inert drug-like and bioavailable compound (clozapine-N-oxide)
Following administration of 10.0 mg/kg CNO, pharmacokinetic analyses replicated recent reports of back-conversion to clozapine
papers.condition: CNO at standard DREADD dose showing clozapine back-conversion
papers.scope_population: Rats and mice in drug discrimination task

papers.value_source_sentence: Systemic delivery of low doses of DCZ (1 or 3 μg per kg) enhanced neuronal activity via hM3Dq within minutes
papers.scope_population: hM3Dq-expressing neurons in mice and monkeys

figure_data.homogeneity_check: Comparable: All measuring effective doses of DREADD actuators. CNO at 10 mg/kg vs DCZ at 1-3 μg/kg represents a ~3000-fold dose reduction. However, direct potency comparison requires consideration of receptor binding affinity, not just dose.

evidence_gaps: Limited quantitative comparison data across different Cre driver lines using identical validation protocols
Few studies directly comparing outcomes of optogenetic activation vs loss-of-function for the same circuit question
Sparse data on VIP-Cre line specificity relative to PV-Cre and SST-Cre validation efforts
Limited systematic comparison of closed-loop vs open-loop optogenetic outcomes for the same behavioral paradigm
Few studies examining how different opsin expression levels affect experimental conclusions about interneuron function
